items.title: Admin Assistant Allegedly Scams Nearly 190 Housemen Out Of RM47,000 At HTA
items.summary: An admin assistant allegedly conned at least 187 housemen in Tunku Azizah Hospital’s paeds department out of over RM47,000 over 2.5 years since Sept 2023. Her two-prong scam: RM900 “refund” for “overpaid” emoluments and RM45 “fee” for a compulsory course. The post Admin Assistant Allegedly Scams Nearly 190 Housemen Out Of RM47,000 At HTA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: From Caregiver To Patient: A Nurse’s Call For Safer Chemotherapy Handling Practices — Parameswari Vadivalu
items.summary: Every nurse and health care worker deserves a safe working environment. Protecting those who care for others is not only a professional obligation but a moral imperative. The post From Caregiver To Patient: A Nurse’s Call For Safer Chemotherapy Handling Practices — Parameswari Vadivalu appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Long Overdue GP Fee Revision Strengthens Primary Care — MMA
items.summary: MMA will continue to advocate for a structured and consistent approach to future revisions, including better alignment across the system. The post Long Overdue GP Fee Revision Strengthens Primary Care — MMA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Breaking The Cycle: Mentorship Over Authoritarianism In Malaysian Medicine — Houseman
items.summary: Authoritarian systems create space for bullying and intimidation, eroding confidence and potentially compromising patient safety. The post Breaking The Cycle: Mentorship Over Authoritarianism In Malaysian Medicine — Houseman appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MOH Moves To ‘Optimise’ Drug Dispensing As Supply Risks Loom
items.summary: MOH's Pharmaceutical Services Programme is tightening medicine dispensing as global cost pressures rise, saying it's "optimising dispensing practices based on clinical needs" and "reviewing patients' existing medication supply to avoid duplication". The post MOH Moves To ‘Optimise’ Drug Dispensing As Supply Risks Loom appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Middle East War Raises Concerns For Malaysia’s Health Care System — Dr Hoomashini Gunasegaran, Dr Sean Thum & Dr Tharani Loganathan
items.summary: Malaysia does have buffers, including energy revenues and existing subsidy frameworks, but these alone are not sufficient. What is needed is a more anticipatory approach that protects access to health care and nutrition while strengthening system resilience. The post Middle East War Raises Concerns For Malaysia’s Health Care System — Dr Hoomashini Gunasegaran, Dr Sean Thum & Dr Tharani Loganathan appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Bully Or Build? Teach Or Tear Down?: The Specialist’s Role In Shaping Culture — Dr Timothy Cheng
items.summary: Doctors do not expect unlimited freedom, but they do expect to be treated as professionals. I’ve caught myself thinking before, “Why this also you don’t know?". But then I remind myself – not everyone is working in the same environment as I am. The post Bully Or Build? Teach Or Tear Down?: The Specialist’s Role In Shaping Culture — Dr Timothy Cheng appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: We Finally Solved Malaysia’s Doctor ‘Oversupply’: 5,000 Posts, 529 Doctors — Clinician
items.summary: If this trajectory of declining housemen numbers continues, we must ask an uncomfortable question: Are we heading back to the 1960s and 1970s, when we had to import doctors from India, Pakistan, Egypt, or Myanmar just to keep the system running? The post We Finally Solved Malaysia’s Doctor ‘Oversupply’: 5,000 Posts, 529 Doctors — Clinician appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Concerns Regarding Cut To Ophthalmology Master’s Programme Candidates — Medical Officer
items.summary: The 2026/27 intake for the ophthalmology Master's programme will be reportedly reduced from 60 to 40 candidates this year. Currently, there are only 318 ophthalmologists in public service, reflecting a significant shortfall of over 300 specialists. The post Concerns Regarding Cut To Ophthalmology Master’s Programme Candidates — Medical Officer appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: New Alzheimer’s Drug In Malaysia May Slow Disease Progression By Eight Years
items.summary: Lecanemab, a new Alzheimer's drug recently approved in Malaysia, shows great promise for patients with early Alzheimer’s by slowing disease progression, potentially by over eight years. The treatment doesn't cure disease, but buys time for patients. The post New Alzheimer’s Drug In Malaysia May Slow Disease Progression By Eight Years appeared first on CodeBlue .
